prompt: Construct a Meridian Academic Publishing & Fulfillment Portal supporting offline-first account access for students, instructors, academic staff, and administrators who manage academic content submissions, reviews, and physical issue fulfillment from a single web experience. The Dioxus-based UI presents a role-aware dashboard after local username/password login, with clear navigation to User Center, Orders, Submissions, and After-Sales. Users can edit profiles, manage US shipping addresses with a single default address, set invoice titles for printable receipts, and control notification preferences limited to in-app inbox banners (email/SMS options appear but remain disabled in offline mode). Students and instructors create multi-round submissions using guided templates (forms plus file attachments), can resubmit up to 10 times before the stated deadline, and always see version history with timestamps in MM/DD/YYYY and 12-hour time; downloads apply visible watermarks showing the requester's name and time. Order & fulfillment screens support subscription periods (monthly, quarterly, annual), line-item quantities, split/merge when an order contains multiple series, and a reconciliation view that compares expected vs. actual receipt per issue, logging missing issues, reshipments, delays, discontinuations, and edition changes with a required reason. Reviews and after-sales flows let users post an initial review plus one follow-up within 14 days, attach up to 6 images (5 MB each), and track return/refund/exchange cases through statuses from Submitted to In Review, Awaiting Evidence, Arbitrated, Approved/Denied, and Closed, with clear SLA timers (first response within 2 business days, resolution target 7 business days). The backend uses Rocket to expose REST-style endpoints consumed by the Dioxus frontend over the same local network or localhost, keeping the architecture decoupled while remaining fully offline-runnable. MySQL persists users, roles, permissions, orders, fulfillment events, submission versions, review records, and after-sales cases, with RBAC enforcing menu/button/data-scope access and an auditable trail of permission changes (who changed what and when). Authentication is local-only with salted password hashing and 30-minute idle session expiry; password recovery is handled by admin-issued one-time reset tokens printed or copied in person, expiring after 60 minutes. Account deletion performs a 30-day soft-delete hold to prevent accidental loss, while Export My Data generates a local downloadable archive limited to the requester's records. Content metadata governance runs on save: title/summary/tags/keywords are validated for length limits (title 120 chars, summary 500), SEO fields are generated from existing text using deterministic rules, and sensitive-word detection uses a local dictionary with replace-or-block policies configurable by admins; blocked content enters a review workflow requiring academic staff approval before publication. File handling enforces format allowlists (PDF/DOCX/PNG/JPG), size limits (25 MB per file), and basic safety checks via file signature verification and checksum logging rather than cloud scanning. Payments are implemented as a pluggable offline module: by default the system records Cash/Check/On-Account transactions with idempotent posting rules, escrow-style holds, refunds, and nightly reconciliation reports; any third-party payment options are explicitly disabled unless an offline-compatible connector is installed, and abnormal-order interception flags patterns such as unusually high quantities or repeated refunds for manual review. Frontend stack
